domain_knowledge: Chemprop uses message passing neural networks to learn molecular representations from graph-structured inputs; the spectral features component extends this architecture to handle infrared spectral prediction tasks.
Spectral features in this context are learned representations that encode infrared spectral properties from molecular graphs, distinct from traditional hand-crafted molecular descriptors.
The chemprop-IR spectral features must output tensors of consistent dimensionality to feed downstream prediction layers in the message passing network.
Feature extraction in chemprop follows a standardized pipeline: molecular parsing (SMILES to graph) → node embedding → message passing → feature aggregation.

direct_checks: verify file exists at github.com/chemprop/chemprop-IR or equivalent public repository URL
verify repository contains README or documentation describing spectral features component
verify repository contains executable Python code or scripts for spectral feature extraction
script_runs: execute spectral feature extraction code with a valid molecular input (SMILES or molecular graph format) and confirm no runtime errors
output_matches_reference: extracted spectral feature vector has expected dimensionality and numeric type (float array or similar) — robust to parameter choices across reasonable molecular inputs
expert_review: chemist or computational chemistry expert: verify that extracted spectral features are chemically sensible and match the named features described in the paper (e.g., functional group indicators, bond type distributions)
expert: confirm that spectral feature construction aligns with standard infrared spectroscopy principles and the chemprop-IR methodology
expert: review whether the implementation correctly translates molecular structure (via SMILES or graph) into feature space claimed in the paper

cmd: cd {inputs.chemprop_ir_repo} && python -c "import sys; sys.path.insert(0, '.'); from chemprop.features import SpectralFeatures; from chemprop.data import MoleculeDataset; molecules = open('{inputs.test_molecules}').read().strip().split('\n'); dataset = MoleculeDataset([{'smiles': smi} for smi in molecules]); extractor = SpectralFeatures(); features = extractor.extract(dataset); print('Features shape:', features.shape); print('Features dtype:', features.dtype); print('Features extracted successfully'); import json; json.dump({'shape': str(features.shape), 'dtype': str(features.dtype), 'status': 'success'}, open('/tmp/spectral_features_report.json', 'w'))"
executable.grounding_notes: Unverified by the executable guard — command runs inline code (-c/-e); its API calls cannot be validated against package files. Treat this command as a best-effort inference; verify the entry point and APIs against the real code before running.

task_description: Extract and construct spectral features from molecular structures using the chemprop-IR spectral features component. Produce a feature extraction module that accepts molecular inputs (SMILES or molecular graphs) and outputs the named spectral features as implemented in the chemprop-IR repository.

Synthesis grounding: the following tools/outputs were NOT found in the source paper and are inferred — verify before use: Spectral features tensor or feature matrix extracted from test molecules, Feature extraction module code or validated implementation script, Verification report confirming feature shape, data type, and completeness.
workflow_description: 1. Clone or load the chemprop-IR repository code from github.com/chemprop/chemprop and identify the spectral features component. 2. Parse the feature extraction implementation to understand the named spectral features and their construction logic. 3. Implement or instantiate the spectral feature extraction module with molecular input handling (SMILES/graph parsing). 4. Test the feature extraction on a small set of example molecules to verify that feature tensors are produced in the correct shape and format. 5. Validate that the extracted features match the expected dimensionality and data types defined in the chemprop-IR architecture.
